---
title: "Core Concepts in Magenta Book"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Magenta Book."
book_id: "magenta-book"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-executive-summary:c0003"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-3-evaluation-methods:c0002"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-5-managing-an-evaluation:c0017"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This synthesis draws across the generated notes for *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation* [@hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book].

## Central Logic

Government evaluation should be scoped around decision needs, appropriate methods, robust data, quality assurance, and clear use of findings.

## Concepts

- Process evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Impact evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Value for money evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Theory-based methods: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Experimental and quasi-experimental methods: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Evaluation management: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.

## Links

- [Executive Summary and Evaluation Types](section-01-executive-summary.qmd)
- [Choosing Evaluation Methods](section-02-evaluation-methods.qmd)
- [Managing an Evaluation](section-03-managing-evaluation.qmd)

## References
